title.en: The service interfaces and the data-transport services
title.zh: 服务接口与数据传输服务

blocks.en: Now that we have met the building blocks — stations, APs, the BSS, the distribution system — let's zoom back out and ask a different question: who owns which job? The 802.11 standard answers this with logical service interfaces. It deliberately refuses to dictate how anything is implemented; instead it lists the services a piece of the architecture is responsible for. A service interface is a contract, not a circuit diagram: it tells you that 'this entity promises to provide these jobs', and leaves the wiring to the vendor.
blocks.zh: 我们已经认识了构成网络的各个基本部件——站点、AP、BSS、分布式系统（DS）。现在让我们退一步，换个角度提问：到底是谁负责哪份工作？802.11 标准用“逻辑服务接口”来回答这个问题。标准刻意不规定任何东西具体如何实现，而是列出架构中某个部件所负责提供的那些服务。服务接口是一份契约，而不是电路图：它告诉你“这个实体承诺提供这些工作”，至于内部怎么连线，则交给厂商去决定。

blocks.en: The standard sorts every service into three families. The Station Service (SS) lives in every single STA — including APs, since an AP contains STA functionality — and covers the per-station basics: authentication, deauthentication, data confidentiality, MSDU delivery, plus spectrum and measurement duties (DFS, TPC, radio measurement, DSE). The PCP service (PCPS) is provided by the PCP of a PBSS and covers association, disassociation, reassociation, and optional QoS scheduling. The Distribution System Service (DSS) is provided by the DS and covers the jobs that need a network behind the AP: distribution, integration, the three association-related services, and DSE.
blocks.zh: 标准把每一项服务都归入三大家族。站点服务（Station Service，SS）存在于每一个 STA 之中——包括 AP，因为 AP 本身就含有 STA 功能——它涵盖每个站点都需要的基础工作：认证、解除认证、数据机密性、MSDU 投递，再加上频谱与测量类职责（DFS、TPC、无线电测量、DSE）。PCP 服务（PCPS）由 PBSS 中的 PCP 提供，涵盖关联、解除关联、重关联以及可选的 QoS 调度。分布式系统服务（DSS）由 DS 提供，涵盖那些需要 AP 背后有一张网络才能完成的工作：分发、整合、三种与关联相关的服务，以及 DSE。

blocks.en: A handy mnemonic: SS = 'what every radio must do by itself', DSS = 'what only a network-of-APs can do', PCPS = 'a PBSS-flavoured subset of the two'. The PCPS jobs are simply a subset of services already found in the SS and DSS — the PCP just plays the coordinating role in a peer-to-peer PBSS.
blocks.zh: 一个好记的口诀：SS = “每台电台自己必须完成的事”，DSS = “只有一张由多个 AP 组成的网络才能做到的事”，PCPS = “前两者的 PBSS 风味子集”。PCPS 的各项工作其实就是 SS 与 DSS 中已有服务的一个子集——PCP 只是在对等式的 PBSS 中扮演协调者的角色。

caption.en: The three service families. SS sits inside every station; DSS is provided by the distribution system; PCPS is a PBSS-specific subset.
caption.zh: 三大服务家族。SS 存在于每个站点内部；DSS 由分布式系统提供；PCPS 是 PBSS 专有的子集。

blocks.en: With ownership clear, look at what the data-transport services actually do. The headline job is moving MSDUs (the payloads handed down from upper layers) between stations. Distribution is the DS's core service: when STA 1 sends a frame to STA 4 across the DS, the 'input' AP hands a MAC service tuple to the DS, and the DS delivers it to the right 'output' AP, which then transmits it onward. Crucially, 802.11 never specifies how the DS routes that tuple — it only requires the association services to feed the DS enough information to find the correct output point. When the recipient lives on a wired (non-802.11) LAN, the output point is a portal instead of an AP, and the Integration service performs any needed media or address-space translation. A third transport service, QoS traffic scheduling, picks which queued frame to send at each TXOP based on user priority and TSPEC.
blocks.zh: 职责归属清楚之后，来看数据传输服务究竟在做什么。最核心的工作是在站点之间搬运 MSDU（即从上层交下来的数据载荷）。“分发（Distribution）”是 DS 的核心服务：当 STA 1 经由 DS 向 STA 4 发送一帧时，“入口”AP 把一个 MAC 服务元组交给 DS，DS 再把它投递到正确的“出口”AP，由后者继续向外发送。关键在于，802.11 从不规定 DS 如何路由这个元组——它只要求关联类服务向 DS 提供足够的信息，以便 DS 找到正确的出口点。当接收方位于有线（非 802.11）局域网上时，出口点就不是 AP 而是“门户（Portal）”，此时由“整合（Integration）”服务完成所需的介质或地址空间转换。第三种传输服务是 QoS 流量调度，它在每个 TXOP 上根据用户优先级和 TSPEC 决定发送哪一个排队的帧。

blocks.en:
flowchart LR
    STA1[STA 1] -->|frame| APin[Input AP / STA 2]
    APin -->|MAC service tuple| DS[(Distribution System)]
    DS -->|to right output| APout[Output AP / STA 3]
    APout -->|frame| STA4[STA 4]
    DS -->|recipient on wired LAN| Portal[Portal: Integration]
    Portal -->|translate media + address| Wired[Non-802.11 LAN]
blocks.zh:
flowchart LR
    STA1[STA 1] -->|帧| APin[入口 AP / STA 2]
    APin -->|MAC 服务元组| DS[(分布式系统)]
    DS -->|送往正确出口| APout[出口 AP / STA 3]
    APout -->|帧| STA4[STA 4]
    DS -->|接收方在有线网| Portal[门户: 整合]
    Portal -->|转换介质+地址| Wired[非 802.11 局域网]

blocks.en: The connectivity services tie a station to the network and let it move. They are best understood through mobility. No-transition means the station stays put or wanders within one BSS — plain association is enough. BSS-transition means moving between BSSs inside the same ESS — that needs reassociation, which re-points the DS's STA-to-AP mapping (and a fast BSS transition sets up data state before the move rather than after). ESS-transition — hopping to a BSS in a different ESS — is supported only loosely: 802.11 cannot guarantee upper-layer connections survive it. Tearing down is the controlled flip side: disassociation voids the STA-to-AP mapping. It is a notification, not a request — either party can send it, and it cannot be refused (except when protected-management-frame integrity fails).
blocks.zh: 连通性服务把一个站点接入网络，并让它能够移动。理解它们最好的切入点是“移动性”。无切换（no-transition）指站点静止不动，或只在同一个 BSS 内移动——此时普通的“关联”就足够了。BSS 切换（BSS-transition）指在同一个 ESS 内的不同 BSS 之间移动——这需要“重关联（Reassociation）”，它会重新指向 DS 中“站点—AP”的映射关系（而“快速 BSS 切换”会在移动之前、而非之后就建立好数据状态）。ESS 切换（ESS-transition）——跳到另一个 ESS 中的 BSS——只得到松散的支持：802.11 无法保证上层连接能在此过程中存活。拆除连接则是受控的另一面：“解除关联（Disassociation）”会作废“站点—AP”的映射。它是一种通知，而非请求——任何一方都可以发出，且对方无法拒绝（除非协商了管理帧保护且其完整性校验失败）。

summary.en: Multiple address spaces, and how ESS / PBSS / IBSS / MBSS differ
summary.zh: 多重地址空间，以及 ESS / PBSS / IBSS / MBSS 的差异

blocks.en: Clause 4.6 makes the address-space abstraction explicit: the WM, the DSM, and an integrated non-802.11 LAN may each operate in different address spaces, but 802.11 itself uses and specifies only the WM address space — the IEEE 802 48-bit address space — so its addresses interoperate with the wider 802 family. Often all three logical address spaces coincide; the architecture merely permits them to be distinct (e.g. a DS using network-layer addressing). STAs within one ESS share one address space, satisfying the DS transparency requirement. Clauses 4.7-4.8 then contrast the network shapes. An IBSS has exactly one BSS and no physical DS, so it has no portal, no integration, and no DSS — only the station service applies, and only Class 1 and Class 2 frames are allowed. A PBSS adds the PCPS: only the PCP transmits DMG Beacons (versus every STA in an IBSS), and the PBSS omits certain DSSs. An MBSS differs from an IBSS in that its multi-hop mesh makes the MAC service appear to deliver MSDUs directly to any member even out of range, and an MBSS can connect to one or more DSs through mesh gates and even serve as a DSM itself.
blocks.zh: 4.6 节把地址空间这一抽象讲明白了：WM、DSM 以及被整合进来的非 802.11 局域网，可能各自工作在不同的地址空间中，但 802.11 自身只使用并只规定 WM 地址空间——即 IEEE 802 的 48 位地址空间——因此它的地址能够与更广泛的 802 家族互通。很多时候这三个逻辑地址空间是重合的；架构只是允许它们各不相同（例如 DS 采用网络层寻址）。同一个 ESS 内的各 STA 共享同一个地址空间，从而满足 DS 的透明性要求。随后 4.7–4.8 节对比了各种网络形态。IBSS 恰好只有一个 BSS，且没有物理 DS，因此它没有门户、没有整合、也没有 DSS——只有站点服务适用，并且只允许 Class 1 和 Class 2 帧。PBSS 额外增加了 PCPS：只有 PCP 发送 DMG 信标帧（而 IBSS 中是每个 STA 都发），并且 PBSS 省去了某些 DSS。MBSS 与 IBSS 的区别在于：它的多跳网状结构使得 MAC 服务看起来能把 MSDU 直接投递给任意成员，哪怕对方不在通信范围内；而且 MBSS 可以通过网状网关连接到一个或多个 DS，甚至自身充当一个 DSM。

4.5.3.2/4.5.3.4/4.5.3.5 — mobility types (no-transition / BSS-transition / ESS-transition), reassociation, disassociation
